objectives: Set up an iOS development workspace and create a runnable project
Navigate the IDE and understand the build/run cycle
Use basic debugging tools to inspect runtime behavior
learning_path.title: iOS Development Setup and First Build

objectives: Write and organize Swift code used in iOS apps
Practice core programming constructs through small exercises
Build and run iterative changes in the IDE
learning_path.title: Swift Fundamentals for iOS

objectives: Create screens and basic UI using the iOS SDK
Connect UI to Swift code and handle user interactions
Run and validate UI behavior through debugging
learning_path.title: UIKit UI Construction and Navigation

objectives: Build simple views and compose UI using SwiftUI
Manage basic state updates and view refresh behavior
Compare workflow differences between SwiftUI and UIKit in the IDE
learning_path.title: SwiftUI Essentials

objectives: Understand RESTful request/response patterns and JSON payloads
Implement a simple network call and parse JSON results
Handle common networking errors during development
learning_path.title: Networking Basics with REST and JSON

objectives: Use concurrency to keep UI responsive during background work
Apply GCD and async/await patterns appropriately
Diagnose concurrency-related issues with debugging tools
learning_path.title: Concurrency for Responsive Apps

objectives: Model and store app data locally using Core Data
Perform basic create/read/update operations
Debug persistence issues and validate stored data
learning_path.title: Local Persistence with Core Data

objectives: Profile runtime performance and identify bottlenecks
Investigate memory and CPU issues using instruments
Apply debugging workflows to confirm improvements
learning_path.title: Profiling and Performance Investigation

objectives: Read and understand Objective-C code in an iOS codebase
Integrate Objective-C components into a Swift project
Debug mixed-language issues during build and runtime
learning_path.title: Objective-C Interoperability in iOS Projects

objectives: Apply configuration management practices to an iOS project
Organize changes to support collaboration and repeatable builds
Use the IDE effectively within a managed workflow
learning_path.title: Configuration Management and Team Workflow

objectives: Relate digital systems concepts to software execution and performance
Connect basic system behavior to debugging observations
Use foundational understanding to reason about runtime issues
learning_path.title: Digital Systems Foundations for App Developers

objectives: Build an end-to-end feature that fetches JSON, displays UI, and persists data
Apply concurrency to coordinate networking and storage safely
Use debugging and profiling to validate correctness and performance
learning_path.title: Capstone Integration: UI + Networking + Persistence

matched_occupation.description: Embedded systems software developers program, implement, document and maintain software to be run on an embedded system.
matched_occupation.label: embedded systems software developer
